subject: Statistics
title: Ensemble Forecasting
description: A quick, practical guide to Ensemble Forecasting.

hook.text: Ask 100 people to guess the weight of an ox. The average beats almost every individual guess.

buildup.text: Ensemble forecasting combines predictions from multiple models. The combination usually wins.

discovery.text: Errors in different models tend to cancel out. One model overshoots where another undershoots.

twist.text: Simple averaging often beats sophisticated weighting schemes. The optimal weights are hard to estimate reliably.

climax.text: Weather centers run dozens of models and average them. M Competition winners almost always use ensembles.

punchline.text: Combine forecasts. The wisdom of models exceeds any individual.

quiz.question: Why do ensemble forecasts outperform individual models?
options: Errors from different models tend to cancel out — one model's overestimate offsets another's underestimate
They always use the best model
